question_text: A teammate makes a mistake that slowed the project.
option_a: I address it gently, focus on fixes, and offer help.
option_b: I criticize them bluntly and assign blame.

question_text: Peer feedback round.
option_a: I give constructive comments and highlight strengths.
option_b: I focus on faults and use a harsh tone.

question_text: A project summary omits a colleague whose work was important to the result.
option_a: I point out their contribution and make sure the summary gives them credit.
option_b: I leave the summary unchanged because the omission benefits my own visibility.

question_text: A partner asks for a short extension after an unexpected family problem.
option_a: I refuse immediately and remind them that their difficulty is not my concern.
option_b: I ask what adjustment would help and negotiate a workable new deadline.

question_text: A customer is confused by instructions that seem obvious to me.
option_a: I explain the steps calmly and check which part remains unclear.
option_b: I show my irritation and tell them they should have understood already.

question_text: Two groups need the same piece of equipment during overlapping time slots.
option_a: I reserve it for my group without discussing the other group's needs.
option_b: I propose a schedule that shares access and accommodates the most urgent work.

question_text: A colleague identifies a serious weakness in a proposal I wrote.
option_a: I thank them for catching it and work with them on a better approach.
option_b: I attack their competence so the group will dismiss their criticism.

question_text: A quiet teammate presents an idea that most of the room initially dislikes.
option_a: I join the dismissal before listening to their reasoning.
option_b: I invite them to explain and make sure the group considers the idea fairly.

question_text: Someone accidentally spills materials I had arranged for an activity.
option_a: I reassure them, help clean up, and focus on restoring the activity.
option_b: I embarrass them in front of everyone and refuse to help with the mess.

question_text: A team must divide several useful tasks and one unpleasant cleanup task.
option_a: I assign the cleanup to the least influential person without asking.
option_b: I suggest rotating or sharing the cleanup so the burden is distributed fairly.

question_text: I hear an unverified rumor that could damage a coworker's reputation.
option_a: I avoid spreading it and encourage others to check facts before judging them.
option_b: I repeat it widely because the story is entertaining.

question_text: The other side in a negotiation makes a concession that gives me leverage.
option_a: I exploit the concession to demand terms I know are needlessly harmful to them.
option_b: I acknowledge the movement and look for terms both sides can sustain.

question_text: A participant is struggling to express an idea in the group's primary language.
option_a: I give them time, ask clarifying questions, and help the group understand.
option_b: I interrupt and exclude them from the discussion because they are taking too long.

question_text: I return a borrowed item with damage that happened while I was using it.
option_a: I hide the damage and blame its condition on the owner.
option_b: I explain what happened, apologize, and offer to repair or replace it.

question_text: Anonymous feedback about my work is written in an unnecessarily harsh tone.
option_a: I separate useful points from the tone and respond without retaliating against the group.
option_b: I write an equally hostile response and try to identify someone to punish.

question_text: A convenient group decision would create extra work for a member who is absent.
option_a: I support it while they are away so they cannot object.
option_b: I raise the impact on them and delay the decision until their perspective is included.

question_text: Someone repeatedly interrupts me while I am explaining a complicated point.
option_a: I ask them firmly but respectfully to let me finish, then invite their response.
option_b: I insult them and interrupt everything they say afterward.

question_text: Two coworkers request my limited help at the same time.
option_a: I help only the coworker who can benefit my career and ignore the other request.
option_b: I explain my capacity, assess urgency, and offer each person a fair form of support.

question_text: Another team succeeds with an approach that competed against my preferred plan.
option_a: I congratulate them and ask what our team can learn from their success.
option_b: I minimize their achievement and look for reasons to deny them recognition.

question_text: A friend apologizes sincerely after making a comment that hurt me.
option_a: I reject the apology so I can keep using the mistake against them.
option_b: I explain the impact, acknowledge the apology, and discuss how to move forward.
